LAW AND MOTION 302, "DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS, INC. AND BAE SYSTEMS SHIP REPAIR, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STAY THIS CASE PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE RELATED LAWSUIT PENDING IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE SOUTHERN DISTRICT OF NEW YORK (SDNY) IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT ALSO RULED ON CERTAIN DISCOVERY AND OTHER ISSUES, AS FULLY REFLECTED ON THE COURT REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT. THE MOVING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bruce E. Van Dalsem, Esq. of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan LLP, Tel. 213.443.3218, for defendant and moving party;
Brian S. Conlon, Esq. and R. Scott Erlewine, Esq. of Phillips Erlewine Given & Carlin LLP, Tel. 415.398.0900, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Owen J. Clements, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3944, for defendant CCSF.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SHIP REPAIR INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar in light of the court's ruling staying this case pending the conclusion of the related lawsuit pending in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

Motion To Dismiss, Or In The Alternative, To Stay The Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bruce E. Van Dalsem, Esq. of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan LLP, Tel. 213.443.3218, for defendant and moving party;
Brian S. Conlon, Esq. and R. Scott Erlewine, Esq. of Phillips Erlewine Given & Carlin LLP, Tel. 415.398.0900, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Owen J. Clements, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3944, for defendant CCSF.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SHIP REPAIR INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss, Or In The Alternative, To Stay The Proceedings as follows: "Defendants BAE Systems, Inc. and BAE Systems Ship Repair, Inc.'s motion to dismiss is denied and its alternative motion to stay this case pending the conclusion of the related lawsuit pending in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York (SDNY) is granted. The claims asserted by plaintiff Puglia Engineering, Inc. in this case are subject to a valid and enforceable forum selection clause providing that any action related to the Purchase Agreement must be brought in the SDNY. By stipulating that the SDNY court can apply California law in deciding the two California Corporations Code claims filed by Puglia notwithstanding the choice of law provision in the Purchase Agreement, the BAE defendants satisfied their burden of showing that enforcement of the forum selection clause will not diminish unwaivable California statutory rights. (Verdugo v. Alliantgroup, L.P. (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 141,145, 158). Puglia's reliance on Hall v. Superior Court (1983) 150 Cal. App. 411 to argue that the forum selection clause is per se unenforceable lacks merit since the conflation of the forum selection clause and choice of law issues in Hall has been rejected by subsequent and better-reasoned decisions, most notably Verdugo. New York is a suitable forum for all of Puglia's claims because the Port of San Francisco has consented to jurisdiction in New York and defendant San Francisco Ship Repair, Inc. was the subject matter of the Purchase Agreement and is now owned by Puglia. This case is stayed, not dismissed, for two reasons. First, it is possible that the SDNY case will not resolve all claims that were or could have been asserted in this case. Second, to guard against the possibility that the SDNY court refuses to apply California law in resolving Puglia's California Corporations Code claims. In that unlikely event, Puglia is invited to file a motion to lift the stay." The Court also ruled on certain discovery and other issues, as fully reflected on the court reporter's transcript. The moving party shall submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
